threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look puzzled when the bus driver shouts 'Next stop, Manse Road!' like it’s the most normal thing in the world.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last week, and a group sang 'Wonderwall' off-key the whole way. Only in South London do night rides turn into mini concerts.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I spotted a kid selling homemade lemonade by the canal while their grandma watched from a folding chair—that's when it clicked.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how the U-Bahn stations here have ad campaigns featuring local artists? Always something fresh, never the same boring corporate stuff.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they don’t flinch at the BART delays but still know the best taco truck to hit after 10 PM.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy dressed as a banana argue with a pigeon over a seat on the 5-Fulton. Only in SF do you get that kind of morning entertainment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm lies in the little plazas filled with locals enjoying vermut on a weekday afternoon.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their struggle with the metro ticket machines—still staring like it's a Rubik's Cube before heading to the counter.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still expect the Tube to run on time. Locals know it's a game of patience and improvisation here.
Last night on the N68, a man carried a cardboard cutout of Duran Duran. He told everyone he was their 'new bassist' and started singing 'Hungry Like the Wolf.'

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: You know you’re in Neukölln when the guy at the corner bakery knows your order and the local park turns into an open-air gallery every weekend.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that doubles as an art gallery—graffiti-covered walls and all, serving the best döner in town.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can name the bus route by the number of people waiting at 16th and Mission, even in the rain.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading passengers on a crowded 27 bus today. He had an old guitar and a killer voice—made the delays feel like a concert.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the locals love the noisy terraces, but it's the late-night trash collection that keeps us up. You notice it after a while.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their oversized map, looking lost at the local markets, while the rest of us know shortcuts through the alleys.
